evals.prompt: I already have an implementation plan for a medium-sized TypeScript bugfix. Should this go to Cody, Pluto, or a Task agent, and what model should I use?
evals.expected_output: Routes to an implementation-oriented worker, keeps Pluto out of the coding lane, prefers a Haiku-first implementation path, and gives an actionable apply-now route.
expectations: Chooses Cody or an implementation-capable Task worker instead of Pluto
Uses haiku as the delegated model tier or explicitly frames sonnet as escalation only
Provides an exact apply-now tool call or shell command

evals.prompt: I want an independent architecture and rollout review for this system change. Should Maggy keep it, use Pluto, or use a Task agent? Pick the model too.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Pluto or an opus-grade review path and explains why an implementation worker is the wrong fit.
expectations: Uses Pluto, code-review, or Maggy-local rather than Cody
Uses opus as the delegated or retained model tier
Explains that this is planning/review work rather than implementation

evals.prompt: I need a quick read-only scan of the repo to identify likely auth and routing touchpoints. Which worker and model should handle that?
evals.expected_output: Routes to read-only exploration on haiku and does not overspend on a premium model.
expectations: Chooses the explore Task agent or an equivalent exploration path
Uses haiku as the delegated model tier
Frames the work as read-only exploration rather than implementation

evals.prompt: If I mention Cody or Pluto by name, explain what each one is for in my current local setup and say which one fits a code review request.
evals.expected_output: Explains the Cody/Pluto role map from local-profile artifacts, distinguishes shell profiles from Task-agent types, and routes code review away from Cody.
expectations: States that Cody and Pluto are shell profiles rather than Task-agent types
Explains Cody as implementation-focused and Pluto as planning/review-focused
Routes code review to Pluto, code-review, or Maggy rather than Cody

evals.prompt: Can you use Cody as the general-purpose Task agent for a planning session, or is there a better route and model?
evals.expected_output: Rejects the shell-profile-vs-Agent conflation, keeps planning out of Cody, and proposes the correct worker/model pair.
expectations: Explicitly says Cody is not a Task-agent type
Does not route planning work to Cody
Provides a corrected route with an explicit model tier

evals.prompt: This is a tiny one-file local fix with an explicit verification script. Should I use Goofy, Cody, or a Task agent?
evals.expected_output: Routes to Goofy as a local worker only because the task is tiny, explicit, and easy to verify.
expectations: Chooses Goofy or explicitly identifies a local-worker route
Uses local as the model/worker tier rather than haiku/sonnet/opus
Explains that the task is safe because it is narrow and easy to verify
Explains that preferring Goofy here reduces cost relative to remote workers

evals.prompt: Should I send planning and routing work to Goofy since it runs locally and avoids Anthropic limits?
evals.expected_output: Rejects Goofy for planning/routing and routes to Maggy or Pluto instead.
expectations: Does not route planning/routing work to Goofy
Explains that Goofy is for narrow explicit work rather than planning
Provides a corrected route to Maggy, Pluto, or an opus-grade review path
